items.title: Hypothesis testing decision flow
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A["State H0 and H1"] --> B["Choose alpha and test"]
  B --> C["Collect data, compute test statistic"]
  C --> D["Compute p-value under H0"]
  D --> E{"p <= alpha?"}
  E -->|yes| F["Reject H0"]
  E -->|no| G["Fail to reject H0"]
items.caption: The procedure is fixed before seeing data: hypotheses, α, and test type determine the rejection rule.

items.title: Type I and Type II errors
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  H0T["H0 true"] --> R["Reject H0"] --> E1["Type I error rate alpha"]
  H0T --> NR["Fail to reject H0"] --> OK1["Correct retention"]
  H0F["H0 false"] --> R2["Reject H0"] --> OK2["Correct detection power"]
  H0F --> NR2["Fail to reject H0"] --> E2["Type II error rate beta"]
items.caption: Wrong rejections when H₀ is true are Type I errors; wrong non-rejections when H₀ is false are Type II errors.

items.title: t-test intuition: signal vs noise
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  num["xbar - mu0: signal"] --> div["divide by"]
  den["s / sqrt(n): noise scale"] --> div
  div --> t["t statistic"]
  t --> dist["compare to t-distribution"]
  dist --> p["p-value"]
items.caption: The t statistic standardizes how many standard errors the sample mean is from the null value.
